originalTitle: US to end funding of South Africa's HIV programmes over claims of Afrikaner persecution
title: US to end funding of South Africa's HIV programmes over claims of Afrikaner persecution

originalTitle: UK law enforcement destroyed my reputation and integrity, ex-Nigerian oil minister tells BBC
title: Finding the Truth: A Story About Fairness
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly reporter from Africa is here with an important story. Sometimes, grown-ups have big jobs and big responsibilities. When someone has a big job, like helping to run a country, it's super important that they follow all the rules and are always fair. Today, we have a story about a very important lady from Nigeria. Her name is Madam Diezani Alison-Madueke. She used to have a very, very big job helping Nigeria. Let's learn about what happened and why it's so important to always try and find the truth.

Who is Madam Diezani?

Imagine your country needs a lot of things to work every day. Cars need fuel, planes need fuel, and many things we use, like plastic toys, are made from something called oil! Oil is like a special treasure deep under the ground. Nigeria is a country in Africa that has lots of this oil. Madam Diezani was once in charge of making sure Nigeria's oil was looked after properly. She was like the head teacher of the whole oil school! This was a very important job because the money from selling oil helps build schools, hospitals, and roads for everyone in Nigeria. Did you know that Nigeria is one of the biggest countries in Africa that has oil? The money from oil helps many people. Imagine if your school had a special treasure that helped buy new books and play equipment for everyone!

What Are Rules and Fairness?

In school, you have rules, right? Like no running in the corridors, or share your toys. Rules help everyone play nicely and safely. When grown-ups have big jobs, they have even bigger rules to follow. The most important rule is to be fair. Being fair means treating everyone equally and making choices that are good for everyone, not just for one person or a few friends. It’s like when you play a game, and everyone gets a turn. That's fair!

A Big Problem Arose

Once upon a time, some people said that Madam Diezani did something that was not fair. They said she took special 'gifts' from other grown-ups who worked with oil. These special gifts are sometimes called 'bribes.' What's a bribe? Imagine if your friend wanted you to pick them first for a game, and they offered you their favourite sweet. If you pick them just because of the sweet, instead of picking who is best for the team, that's not fair! It’s like breaking the rules of fairness. People thought Madam Diezani might have done something like that with her big job.

Finding Out the Truth – The Trial

When someone is accused of breaking big rules, especially in a big job, there's a special way to find out the truth. It's called a 'trial.' A trial is like a very serious meeting where everyone gets to tell their side of the story. There are special grown-ups called 'judges' and 'lawyers' who help listen to everything. The judge is like the referee in a game, making sure everything is fair and following the rules. This trial for Madam Diezani took a very, very long time – six whole months! That's like half a year! Did you know that judges often wear special long black robes when they are working? This makes them look very important and helps everyone know they are in charge of finding the truth!

The Big Decision: Not Guilty!

After all that time, listening to all the stories and looking at all the information, the judge made a big decision. The judge said that Madam Diezani was 'cleared.' This means she was found 'not guilty.' It means that after looking at everything, the judge decided that she did not take those special gifts or break the rules of fairness. It's like when you are accused of eating all the cookies, but then your mum finds the real cookie monster (maybe your brother!) and you are cleared! You didn't do it!

Why This Made Madam Diezani Sad

Even though Madam Diezani was cleared and found not guilty, she felt sad. Why? Because when people say you did something wrong, even if you didn't, it makes others think bad things about you. It can hurt your 'good name.' She felt that people stopped trusting her and thinking she was honest. This feeling, that her good name was hurt, is called 'reputation' and 'integrity.' It means how people see you and if they believe you always do the right thing. It's important to remember that just because someone is accused, it doesn't mean they are guilty. We must wait for the truth. Did you know that having a good reputation is like having a shining shield? It helps people trust you and know you are a good friend!

Why This Story Matters to All of Us

This story is important for all of us, even for young children like you. It teaches us that being fair is very, very important, especially when you have a big job. It also teaches us that it takes time to find the truth, and we should always wait for the truth before we decide if someone did something wrong. Everyone deserves to be treated fairly, and everyone deserves to have their 'good name' protected. When someone is found 'not guilty,' it means justice was done. So, let’s always remember to be fair, be kind, and always look for the truth!
summary: Madam Diezani Alison-Madueke, a former important leader from Nigeria, was accused of breaking rules but was found innocent after a long time, showing everyone the importance of truth and fairness.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us that everyone deserves to be treated fairly, and we must always wait for the truth before we decide if someone did something wrong, even if they have a big job.
